evidence: Organization missing recommended: logo, sameAs, contactPoint
WebPage missing recommended: dateModified, isPartOf, primaryImageOfPage, inLanguage
FAQPage schema has 8 Questions but page shows ~15 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
15 em-dashes (4.38 per 400 words; target <1)
21 tricolons (7.67/500 words; target ≤2)
Human voice vs committee 1/10 — Reads like a template filled in by a content generator; no individual voice anywhere.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) in 2026 is the discipline of making content discoverable, citable, and preferred in AI-generated answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) in 2026 is the discipline of making content discoverable, citable, and preferred in AI-generated answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: I've spent the last year watching our content get quoted by ChatGPT—and ignored by Perplexity. Here's what actually moved the needle.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 1/10: Reads like a template filled in by a content generator; no individual voice anywhere.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 1/10 — Every claim is hedged, generic, and uncontroversial; no stake in the ground.

suggestedPatch.target: Structured data, clear definitions, and authoritative content are key signals for LLM-based engines.
suggestedPatch.before: Structured data, clear definitions, and authoritative content are key signals for LLM-based engines.
suggestedPatch.after: Schema markup is overrated for AEO—in our tests, a single well-cited statistic outperformed a full DefinedTerm rollout.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 1/10: Every claim is hedged, generic, and uncontroversial; no stake in the ground.

evidence: Unexpected phrasing 1/10 — Phrasing is entirely predictable AI boilerplate: 'discipline of', 'intent fulfillment', 'citation-worthy signals'.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO optimizes for structured evidence, clarity, and signals that LLMs recognize as authoritative.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO optimizes for structured evidence, clarity, and signals that LLMs recognize as authoritative.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO is less about pleasing a crawler and more about writing something an LLM feels safe plagiarizing.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasing" scored 1/10: Phrasing is entirely predictable AI boilerplate: 'discipline of', 'intent fulfillment', 'citation-worthy signals'.

evidence: Specific citations (names/places/prices/dates) 3/10 — Names engines and vague references but no actual numbers, studies, people, prices, or dated examples.

suggestedPatch.target: Academic and industry research highlight that citation, quotation, and original statistics drive AEO performance.
suggestedPatch.before: Academic and industry research highlight that citation, quotation, and original statistics drive AEO performance.
suggestedPatch.after: Princeton's GEO paper (Aggarwal et al., Nov 2023) found citation insertion boosted source visibility by up to 40% in Perplexity-style responses.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations (names/places/prices/dates)" scored 3/10: Names engines and vague references but no actual numbers, studies, people, prices, or dated examples.

evidence: Point of view vs both-sides survey 2/10 — Surveys every angle neutrally without taking a position.

suggestedPatch.target: While E-E-A-T and helpful content matter in both, AEO demands an even sharper focus on authority markup, citation-worthiness, and clear factual structuring.
suggestedPatch.before: While E-E-A-T and helpful content matter in both, AEO demands an even sharper focus on authority markup, citation-worthiness, and clear factual structuring.
suggestedPatch.after: E-E-A-T is a red herring for AEO; LLMs don't care who you are, only whether your sentence is quotable out of context.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides survey" scored 2/10: Surveys every angle neutrally without taking a position.

evidence: Quotability 1/10 — Not a single line has the sharpness or surprise you'd text to a colleague.

suggestedPatch.target: Mastering AEO requires optimizing for authority signals, schema markup, and phrasing that aligns with LLM source-picking logic.
suggestedPatch.before: Mastering AEO requires optimizing for authority signals, schema markup, and phrasing that aligns with LLM source-picking logic.
suggestedPatch.after: The trick isn't writing for humans or robots—it's writing the one sentence a lazy LLM will paste into its answer.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotability" scored 1/10: Not a single line has the sharpness or surprise you'd text to a colleague.

evidence: Intro earns attention 1/10 — Opens with a dictionary definition stacked on a second dictionary definition; zero hook.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) in 2026 is the discipline of making content discoverable, citable, and preferred in AI-generated answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) in 2026 is the discipline of making content discoverable, citable, and preferred in AI-generated answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Last month our competitor got cited 14 times in ChatGPT answers. We got zero. The difference wasn't content quality—it was three lines of schema.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 1/10: Opens with a dictionary definition stacked on a second dictionary definition; zero hook.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — The section on brand visibility factors recycles the same trio of signals (schema, evidence, authority) used in every other section without adding concrete numbers, named studies, or real-world brand examples. 'Establish authority' and 'embed clear evidence' are pure abstraction. The cited references ([geo-arxiv], [semrush-aeo-2024]) are never unpacked into actual findings or figures anywhere in the post, leaving the specificity claim hollow. The AEO vs SEO table is the post's strongest specificity asset, but the body copy surrounding it repeatedly floats in the same vague language.

suggestedPatch.target: Which Factors Drive Brand Visibility in AI Answers? Academic and industry research highlight that citation, quotation, and original statistics drive AEO performance. Brands who structure content with schema.org markup, embed clear evidence, and establish authority increase their likelihood of being cited in AI answer boxes.
suggestedPatch.before: Which Factors Drive Brand Visibility in AI Answers? Academic and industry research highlight that citation, quotation, and original statistics drive AEO performance. Brands who structure content with schema.org markup, embed clear evidence, and establish authority increase their likelihood of being cited in AI answer boxes.
suggestedPatch.after: What actually moves the needle for AEO citations? The arXiv GEO study (2311.09735) found that adding citations increased AI citation-share by up to 30%, while fluency rewrites lifted it 15%. For B2B SaaS brands, adding a schema.org DefinedTerm block to a product glossary page and embedding one original statistic with a named source (e.g., '67% of Perplexity queries now return zero blue links—Semrush, Jan 2026') are the two highest-ROI starting moves.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The section on brand visibility factors recycles the same trio of signals (schema, evidence, authority) used in every other section without adding concrete numbers, named studies, or real-world brand examples. 'Establish authority' and 'embed clear evidence' are pure abstraction. The cited references ([geo-arxiv], [semrush-aeo-2024]) are never unpacked into actual findings or figures anywhere in the post, leaving the specificity claim hollow. The AEO vs SEO table is the post's strongest specificity asset, but the body copy surrounding it repeatedly floats in the same vague language.
